---
title: "nitrix.geometry.differential"
generated: true
signature-hash: b81a51a07746818b
description: "Image spatial-gradient operators."
listing:
  id: nitrix-geometry-differential-symbols
  contents: "nitrix-geometry-differential/*.qmd"
  type: table
  fields: [title, description]
  sort: "title"
  filter-ui: false
  page-size: 500
---

::: {.callout-important title="Generated reference stub — fill me"}
Auto-generated scaffold for `nitrix.geometry.differential`. Fill the prose *outside* the
generated markers; do **not** ship as-is (SPEC §6.5). `check_reference_staleness.py` flags signature drift.
:::

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:__module__ -->
Image spatial-gradient operators.

The directional derivatives $\partial I / \partial x_d$ of a
scalar field along each of its spatial axes -- the gradient
$\nabla M$ that drives both registration recipes (the rigid
Gauss-Newton steepest-descent images $\nabla M \cdot (G_j x)$
and the diffeomorphic-Demons symmetric force
$\tfrac{1}{2}(\nabla F + \nabla(M \circ \varphi))$).

Three separable schemes, all differentiable and all reusing one
cross-correlation engine:

- `"central"` (default) -- the bare central difference
  $(I[+1] - I[-1]) / (2 h)$.  Generalises the private
  central-difference helper used by [`jacobian_displacement`](nitrix-geometry-grid/jacobian_displacement.qmd).
- `"sobel"` -- central difference along the derivative axis,
  $[1, 2, 1] / 4$ smoothing along every other spatial axis
  (noise-robust).
- `"scharr"` -- as Sobel with $[3, 10, 3] / 16$ smoothing
  (better rotational symmetry).

Layout follows the Gaussian smoothing operators: `(..., *spatial)`
with the trailing `spatial_rank` axes spatial.  The result appends a
trailing derivative-direction axis: `(..., *spatial, ndim)`.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen dims:__page__ -->
**Tensor dimensions**

| dim | meaning |
|---|---|
| `...` | anonymous leading (batch) axes |
| `*spatial` | the spatial axes of a grid (variadic) |
| `ndim` | number of spatial dimensions |
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

## Symbols

::: {#nitrix-geometry-differential-symbols}
:::
